     9→# Wire Production Nomad Job Submission for Transflow Healing
    10→
    11→## Problem/Goal
    12→The transflow healing infrastructure has placeholder implementations for production job submission. Currently, the `jobSubmissionHelper` and `fanoutOrchestrator` only work with mock implementations for testing. We need to wire up the actual Nomad job submission to enable real healing workflows with planner/reducer jobs and parallel healing branches.
    13→
    14→## Success Criteria
    15→- [ ] Replace mock job submission with real Nomad client integration
    16→- [ ] Implement HCL template rendering for healing job specs (planner, reducer, llm-exec, etc.)
    17→- [ ] Wire `SubmitAndWaitTerminal` to use `internal/orchestration/submit.go`
    18→- [ ] Add job status monitoring and log retrieval for healing workflows
    19→- [ ] Ensure dynamic variable substitution in job templates (MODEL, TOOLS_JSON, RUN_ID, etc.)
    20→- [ ] Test end-to-end healing workflow with real Nomad job execution
    21→- [ ] Handle job failures and timeouts gracefully with proper error propagation
    22→

    26→- @internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestrator.go:1-150  # Parallel execution logic

content.text: Now I'll gather comprehensive context by examining all the referenced files and related components to understand the current architecture and what needs to be connected.

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"sync"
     7→	"time"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// fanoutOrchestrator implements the FanoutOrchestrator interface
    11→type fanoutOrchestrator struct {
    12→	submitter interface{} // MockJobSubmitter in tests, real submitter in production
    13→}
    14→
    15→// NewFanoutOrchestrator creates a new fanout orchestrator
    16→func NewFanoutOrchestrator(submitter interface{}) FanoutOrchestrator {
    17→	return &fanoutOrchestrator{
    18→		submitter: submitter,
    19→	}
    20→}
    21→
    22→// RunHealingFanout executes parallel healing branches with first-success-wins semantics
    23→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error) {
    24→	if len(branches) == 0 {
    25→		return BranchResult{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("no branches to execute")
    26→	}
    27→
    28→	// Create context for cancellation when first branch succeeds
    29→	fanoutCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
    30→	defer cancel()
    31→
    32→	// Channel to receive results
    33→	resultCh := make(chan BranchResult, len(branches))
    34→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    35→
    36→	// Semaphore to limit parallelism
    37→	sem := make(chan struct{}, maxParallel)
    38→
    39→	// Launch all branches
    40→	for _, branch := range branches {
    41→		wg.Add(1)
    42→		go func(b BranchSpec) {
    43→			defer wg.Done()
    44→
    45→			// Acquire semaphore slot
    46→			select {
    47→			case sem <- struct{}{}:
    48→			case <-fanoutCtx.Done():
    49→				// Context cancelled, branch doesn't execute
    50→				resultCh <- BranchResult{
    51→					ID:     b.ID,
    52→					Status: "cancelled",
    53→					Notes:  "cancelled before execution",
    54→				}
    55→				return
    56→			}
    57→			defer func() { <-sem }() // Release semaphore slot
    58→
    59→			// Execute the branch
    60→			result := o.executeBranch(fanoutCtx, b)
    61→			resultCh <- result
    62→
    63→			// If this branch succeeded and context isn't cancelled, cancel others
    64→			if result.Status == "completed" {
    65→				cancel()
    66→			}
    67→		}(branch)
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	// Collect results
    71→	var allResults []BranchResult
    72→	var winner BranchResult
    73→	foundWinner := false
    74→
    75→	// Wait for all branches to complete or be cancelled
    76→	go func() {
    77→		wg.Wait()
    78→		close(resultCh)
    79→	}()
    80→
    81→	for result := range resultCh {
    82→		allResults = append(allResults, result)
    83→
    84→		// First successful result becomes the winner
    85→		if result.Status == "completed" && !foundWinner {
    86→			winner = result
    87→			foundWinner = true
    88→		}
    89→	}
    90→
    91→	if !foundWinner {
    92→		return BranchResult{}, allResults, fmt.Errorf("all branches failed, no winner")
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	return winner, allResults, nil
    96→}
    97→
    98→// executeBranch executes a single branch and returns the result
    99→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec) BranchResult {
   100→	startTime := time.Now()
   101→
   102→	result := BranchResult{
   103→		ID:        branch.ID,
   104→		Status:    "failed", // Default to failed
   105→		StartedAt: startTime,
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Check if context is already cancelled
   109→	select {
   110→	case <-ctx.Done():
   111→		result.Status = "cancelled"
   112→		result.Notes = "context cancelled"
   113→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   114→		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   115→		return result
   116→	default:
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	// Use type assertion to check if this is a test submitter
   120→	if testSubmitter, ok := o.submitter.(interface {
   121→		SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx context.Context, spec JobSpec) (JobResult, error)
   122→	}); ok {
   123→		spec := JobSpec{
   124→			Name:    branch.ID,
   125→			Type:    branch.Type,
   126→			Inputs:  branch.Inputs,
   127→			Timeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Default timeout
   128→		}
   129→
   130→		jobResult, err := testSubmitter.SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx, spec)
   131→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   132→		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   133→
   134→		if err != nil {
   135→			result.Status = "failed"
   136→			result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("job execution failed: %v", err)
   137→		} else {
   138→			result.JobID = jobResult.JobID
   139→			result.Status = jobResult.Status
   140→			result.Notes = jobResult.Output
   141→		}
   142→
   143→		return result
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	// For production implementation, would use actual job submission
   147→	result.Status = "failed"
   148→	result.Notes = "production job submission not implemented yet"
   149→	result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   150→	result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   151→	return result
   152→}
   153→

content.text: Now let me examine the LLM execution job template and look for interface definitions:

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→// Job submission types for transflow healing workflow
     9→
    10→// JobSpec describes a job to be submitted to the orchestrator
    11→type JobSpec struct {
    12→	Name    string                 `json:"name"`
    13→	Type    string                 `json:"type"`
    14→	HCLPath string                 `json:"hcl_path"`
    15→	EnvVars map[string]string      `json:"env_vars"`
    16→	Timeout time.Duration          `json:"timeout"`
    17→	Inputs  map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    18→}
    19→
    20→// JobResult contains the result of a completed job
    21→type JobResult struct {
    22→	JobID     string            `json:"job_id"`
    23→	Status    string            `json:"status"` // "completed", "failed", "timeout"
    24→	Output    string            `json:"output"`
    25→	Error     string            `json:"error"`
    26→	Duration  time.Duration     `json:"duration"`
    27→	Artifacts map[string]string `json:"artifacts"`
    28→}
    29→
    30→// PlanResult contains the output from a planner job
    31→type PlanResult struct {
    32→	PlanID  string                   `json:"plan_id"`
    33→	Options []map[string]interface{} `json:"options"`
    34→}
    35→
    36→// NextAction describes what the reducer determined should happen next
    37→type NextAction struct {
    38→	Action string `json:"action"`
    39→	Notes  string `json:"notes"`
    40→}
    41→
    42→// BranchSpec describes a healing branch to execute
    43→type BranchSpec struct {
    44→	ID     string                 `json:"id"`
    45→	Type   string                 `json:"type"`
    46→	Inputs map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// BranchResult contains the result of executing a healing branch
    50→type BranchResult struct {
    51→	ID         string        `json:"id"`
    52→	Status     string        `json:"status"`
    53→	JobID      string        `json:"job_id"`
    54→	Notes      string        `json:"notes"`
    55→	StartedAt  time.Time     `json:"started_at"`
    56→	FinishedAt time.Time     `json:"finished_at"`
    57→	Duration   time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    58→}
    59→
    60→// Interface definitions
    61→
    62→// JobSubmissionHelper provides methods for submitting transflow jobs
    63→type JobSubmissionHelper interface {
    64→	SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, config *TransflowConfig, buildError string, workspace string) (*PlanResult, error)
    65→	SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, planID string, results []BranchResult, winner *BranchResult, workspace string) (*NextAction, error)
    66→}
    67→
    68→// FanoutOrchestrator manages parallel execution of healing branches
    69→type FanoutOrchestrator interface {
    70→	RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error)
    71→}
    72→

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→	"strings"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/cli/common"
    12→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/git/provider"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// GitOperationsInterface defines the Git operations needed by the runner
    16→type GitOperationsInterface interface {
    17→	CloneRepository(ctx context.Context, repoURL, branch, targetPath string) error
    18→	CreateBranchAndCheckout(ctx context.Context, repoPath, branchName string) error
    19→	CommitChanges(ctx context.Context, repoPath, message string) error
    20→	PushBranch(ctx context.Context, repoPath, remoteURL, branchName string) error
    21→}
    22→
    23→// RecipeExecutorInterface defines the recipe execution interface
    24→type RecipeExecutorInterface interface {
    25→	ExecuteRecipes(ctx context.Context, workspacePath string, recipeIDs []string) error
    26→}
    27→
    28→// BuildCheckerInterface defines the build check interface
    29→type BuildCheckerInterface interface {
    30→	CheckBuild(ctx context.Context, config common.DeployConfig) (*common.DeployResult, error)
    31→}
    32→
    33→// StepResult represents the result of executing a single step
    34→type StepResult struct {
    35→	StepID   string
    36→	Success  bool
    37→	Message  string
    38→	Duration time.Duration
    39→}
    40→
    41→// TransflowResult represents the overall result of a transflow execution
    42→type TransflowResult struct {
    43→	Success        bool
    44→	WorkflowID     string
    45→	BranchName     string
    46→	CommitSHA      string
    47→	BuildVersion   string
    48→	StepResults    []StepResult
    49→	ErrorMessage   string
    50→	Duration       time.Duration
